note: The Almanac seeds Burnthday's lore about when Widespread Panic tends to play certain songs. Each 'play' note is Burnthday's own words, rendered verbatim with a '— Burnthday' attribution; lyric quotes are short fair-use snippets. Tiers are NOT stored here — they are computed at build time from the 3,132-show setlist.fm cache (ratio + exact binomial p-value). The 'claim' field records the epistemic category of each entry (lyric = a lyric predicts the day; owner = Burnthday vouches for it; behavioral = a strong data pattern he confirms; dredged = a curiosity found by scanning), which the build combines with significance to assign a tier.

entries.lyric: Gimme a kiss there, Tuesday / I'll give you a kiss there, too.
entries.play: The ultimate weapon for rare mid-week Tuesday tour stops.

entries.lyric: Just a-knockin' 'round the zoo / On a Thursday afternoon.
entries.play: Frequently dropped as a Thursday show opener or early first-set rocker to kick off multi-night weekend runs.

entries.lyric: It was six o'clock on Saturday / Henry Parsons died.
entries.play: Heavily favored for Saturday sets, often opening the show or kicking off Set 2 right as the sun goes down around 6:00 PM.

entries.lyric: Singing Flewzy woozy boogie on a Saturday night.
entries.play: A classic, high-octane Saturday night second-set closer or encore designed to leave the crowd dancing.

entries.lyric: Monday morning... wake up, wake up / It's time to go to work again.
entries.play: JB leans heavily into these lines during the outro, making it the perfect cheeky nod when played as a Sunday night closer or encore before everyone heads back to reality.

entries.lyric: Jingle bells, electric lights / Oh, they rattle 'gainst the shopping cart / Even on the fourth of July.
entries.play: A guaranteed highly-anticipated bust-out during the band's annual Fourth of July summer runs.

entries.lyric: Walking in the haze, Sunday's Independence Day / Another good excuse for a celebration.
entries.play: Kept locked and loaded for whenever the Fourth of July holiday lands squarely on a Sunday.

entries.lyric: And the best thing about New Years is the Christmas lights.
entries.play: A beautiful staple that gets a massive crowd roar when dropped during the multi-night New Year's Eve runs.

entries.title: Loose Monday
entries.note: On the band's rare Monday shows, the covers come out to play. These five all turn up on Mondays far more than their overall rate — a loose, off-night streak the numbers happen to like.
Harry Nilsson's novelty tune has a soft spot for Halloween — a fun costume of a cover for the spookiest night on the tour calendar.
It leans Sunday, but only suggestively — a gentle tilt the scan noticed, nothing you'd bet a beer on.
